legs.desc: (Reps per leg x sets). Place one foot behind you on a bench, step the other foot forward, then lower your hips until the front thigh is parallel to the ground. Works quads, glutes, and hamstrings. Focus on keeping the front knee aligned with your toes.

Stand with feet hip-width apart, barbell or dumbbells in hands. Hinge at the hips while keeping a slight bend in the knees, lowering the weight along your legs. Strengthens hamstrings, glutes, and lower back. Keep your spine neutral.
Sit on a leg extension machine and extend your legs until straight, then lower slowly. Isolates the quadriceps. Control the weight to avoid knee strain.
Lie on your back with knees bent, feet flat. Push your hips up toward the ceiling, squeezing glutes at the top. Targets glutes, hamstrings, and lower back. Avoid overarching the lower back.
Performed lying or seated on a hamstring curl machine. Curl your heels toward your glutes, then lower slowly. Isolates the hamstrings. Maintain controlled motion to prevent momentum.
Step one leg forward (or backward), bend knees to lower your body until both thighs are roughly parallel. Push through the front heel to return. Works quads, glutes, hamstrings, and improves balance.

(Reps per leg x sets). Stand on a flat surface or elevated step, push through the balls of your feet to lift heels, then lower slowly. Strengthens calves (gastrocnemius and soleus). Pause at the top for extra contraction.
With a barbell across your shoulders, hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back flat. Return upright. Strengthens hamstrings, glutes, and lower back. Avoid rounding your spine.

legs_stretches.desc: Stand on one leg, pull the other heel toward your glutes.
Keeping hips over feet, gradually lean forward with your arms dangling in front of you, as low as you can go, and hold for 15 seconds. You'll feel the stretch in your hamstrings and calves.

Starting with one foot forward, knee bent at a 90° angle and your other leg straight behind you, torso upright and tailbone tucked, lean forward slightly and optionally raise your arms. Hold for 20-30 seconds, then switch sides. Repeat 3 times per leg.

Sit upright with legs extended, feet together as close to your groin as you can. Relax your knees and let them drop to the floor, and lean forward to feel a stronger stretch. Hold for 15 seconds, then release and repeat three times.

Starting seated with hands behind you, bend both knees at 90° angles, one thigh pointing straight down and the other thigh at a 90° to your body. Pivot on your heels with toes in the air, to switch the pointing of each thigh. Repeat 10-15 times each direction.

Starting seated with both legs stretched in front of you, bench one knee bringing your foot as close to your glutes as possible, and press that knee to the floor, hold for 2 seconds, and return that leg to point forward. Repeat for 10 reps, then switch to other leg.

Start with legs tucked behind you, bum planted between your heels and elbows under your shoulders to hold you up. Gradually lower yourself onto your back, feeling an intense stretch in your hamstrings and knees. Hold for 4-5 deep breaths before walking your arms back under you. If it's too difficult, plant your heels closer together, sitting on your feet.

Start with feet hip-width apart, chest lifted, step with one leg to your side and bend your other leg under you to support. Keep your bent knee aligned with that foot, then push off with this leg to return to the starting position.

push.desc: Lying on a 20° incline bench, press a barbell upward and lower under control. Emphasizes upper chest and front deltoids. Keep shoulders pressed against the bench and avoid flaring elbows excessively.
Sit upright, press dumbbells overhead, then lower to shoulder level. Targets deltoids and triceps. Keep core engaged and avoid leaning back.
Lying on a bench, extend dumbbells or a barbell from forehead to full arm extension. Isolates triceps. Keep elbows stationary and avoid flaring.
e.g. Pushups: Bodyweight exercise, hands on the floor or bench, lower chest toward the ground, then press back up. Works chest, triceps, and shoulders.
Lying flat on a bench, press dumbbells from chest upward, lowering slowly. Works pectorals, shoulders, and triceps. Maintain controlled motion.
Begin with dumbbells at shoulders, palms facing you, rotate palms outward while pressing overhead. Targets all heads of the deltoid. Avoid using momentum.
Two sets of dips, lateral raises or pushup variation set.

push_stretches.desc: Standing tall, arms hanging down by your sides, tip your head to one side, trying to touch your shoulder with your ear. Hold for 10 seconds, then release and repeat on the other side.

Standing tall, arms hanging down by your sides, breat in and lift shoulders towards ears and roll back to pinch shoulder blades behind your back, roll down, then forward to feel stretch at the back of shoulders. Repeat 10 times, then change direction.
Lean forward and place one hand on a chair or table in front of you for support. Swing other arm gengly in a circular motion for 30-60 seconds (changing direction if you choose), then swap arms.

Inhale, lift your arms out to your sides, squeezing your shoulder blades together. Exhale, bringing your arms across your body, stretching the back of your shoulder blades. Repeat 10 times, swapping which arm is on top each repetition.

Stretch one arm out straight, bringing it across your body so that it points slightly to the floor on the other side. Hook your opposing elbow under the outstretched arm above the elbow and pull outstretched arm towards you. Hold for 20 seconds, then swap arms and repeat 3 times per arm.

Reach one arm above and behind your head, reaching down your back. Reach your other arm under behind your back, attempting to clasp your hands together behind your back. Hold for 15 seconds, then swap arms and repeat 3 times per arm.

Start with knees on the ground, touching big toes together and knees spread apart. Reach arms above head and bow forwards towards the floor, reaching arms out in front of you. Bring elbows to the ground and sit back to heels. Hold for 60 seconds.

Lie on one side with knees bent pointing in front of you. Reach your top arm in line with your shoulders above and reach out behind you, keeping knees and hips pointing forward. Repeat several times, then swap sides.

Stand with feet hip-width apart, bend your knees slightly and place each hand on the opposing arms elbow. Keeping stomach against bent knees, bend forward and try to touch your toes, letting your head hang heavy. Hold for 60 seconds or longer.

Starting standing, lift arms to your sides and swing them across your body. Allow one arm to cross under the other and cradle the upper arm in the elbow. Rotate your arms up, trying to place palms together while all twisted up. Hold for 10 seconds, and swap arms. Repeat 3 times.

pull.desc: Sit on a 65° incline bench and perform bicep curls. Emphasizes the long head of the biceps for peak development. Maintain controlled movement.
Place one hand and knee on a bench, pull a dumbbell toward your hip with the other hand. Strengthens lats, rhomboids, and traps. Avoid rotating torso.
Lie on a bench, hold a dumbbell with both hands, and lower it behind your head, then bring it over your chest. Stretches chest and lats. Keep a slight bend in elbows.
Variations; normal (hands shoulder width apart), diamond (hands close, forefinger and thumb touching) and wide (arms twice a shoulder width apart).
Hold dumbbells at your sides and lift shoulders toward ears. Targets trapezius muscles. Pause at the top for maximum contraction.
Similar setup to incline curl, but palms face each other throughout the movement. Works biceps and brachialis.
Lie chest-down on an incline bench and row dumbbells toward your torso. Works upper back and rear delts. Keep shoulders down and back.
Biceps curl variation: 7 reps from bottom to midpoint, 7 reps from midpoint to top, 7 full reps. Maximally fatigues biceps and builds pump.

pull_stretches.desc: Standing tall, arms hanging down by your sides, tip your head to one side, trying to touch your shoulder with your ear. Hold for 10 seconds, then release and repeat on the other side.

Standing tall, arms hanging down by your sides, breat in and lift shoulders towards ears and roll back to pinch shoulder blades behind your back, roll down, then forward to feel stretch at the back of shoulders. Repeat 10 times, then change direction.
Clasping hands together behind the waist, extend elbows backwards, turning palms outwards as you do allowing arms to naturaly raise. Hold the stretch for 10 seconds, then release, repeating 3 times.

Keeping torso upright, extend arms forwards then upwards, rotating shoulders and drawing hands together above head. At the top of the stretch, elbows should be straight and palms should be facing upwards.

Standing facing sideways to a wall, place palm flat against the wall alowing elbow to bend slightly. Rotate body with feed, turning away from the wall allowing elbow to extend. Hold for a few seconds once you feel a stretch then rotate back. Repeat 3 times per arm.

Starting seated, plant both palms behind the back at a distance that allows for near-full elbow extension. Slowly slide butt forward along the ground, keeping palms firmly planted until you feel a stretch. Hold for 5-10 seconds, then release, repeating 3 times.
Lean forward and place one hand on a chair or table in front of you for support. Swing other arm gengly in a circular motion for 30-60 seconds (changing direction if you choose), then swap arms.

Inhale, lift your arms out to your sides, squeezing your shoulder blades together. Exhale, bringing your arms across your body, stretching the back of your shoulder blades. Repeat 10 times, swapping which arm is on top each repetition.

Stretch one arm out straight, bringing it across your body so that it points slightly to the floor on the other side. Hook your opposing elbow under the outstretched arm above the elbow and pull outstretched arm towards you. Hold for 20 seconds, then swap arms and repeat 3 times per arm.

Reach one arm above and behind your head, reaching down your back. Reach your other arm under behind your back, attempting to clasp your hands together behind your back. Hold for 15 seconds, then swap arms and repeat 3 times per arm.

Start with knees on the ground, touching big toes together and knees spread apart. Reach arms above head and bow forwards towards the floor, reaching arms out in front of you. Bring elbows to the ground and sit back to heels. Hold for 60 seconds.

Lie on one side with knees bent pointing in front of you. Reach your top arm in line with your shoulders above and reach out behind you, keeping knees and hips pointing forward. Repeat several times, then swap sides.

knees.desc: Perform a single-leg squat and step the other foot in multiple directions like the hours on a clock (forward, 45°, 90°, 135°, backward, 225°).
Jump as high as you can go, then when you land, squat as low as you can. Perform five jumps for six sets, resting for a minute or so between each set
(30 seconds per set). Step forward or backward and perform lunges using one leg at a time while keeping the other leg stationary. Emphasizes unilateral strength and balance.

(30 seconds per set). Lie on your back, knees bent, lift one leg off the floor, and raise hips with the other leg driving. Isolates glutes and hamstrings on the working leg. Focus on controlled lift and squeeze at the top.
Intended as an optional finisher, spring up a hill as fast as you can in 6 second bursts. Rest for at least a minute between sets.
